name: Stars and the Solar System
subject: physics
summary: The Sun, planets, moons, asteroids, comets — our cosmic neighbourhood and how celestial objects move.

crushed.title: Sun = nearest star
crushed.text: A ball of hot plasma. Provides light & heat. 8 planets orbit it in elliptical paths.

crushed.title: 8 planets in order
crushed.text: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune. My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Nuts.

crushed.title: Earth is special
crushed.text: Right distance from Sun for liquid water. One moon. Life exists here.

crushed.title: Small bodies
crushed.text: Asteroids (rocky, Mars-Jupiter belt), Comets (icy, long elliptical orbits with tails), Meteors (shooting stars).

crushed.title: Moon phases
crushed.text: Moon doesn't make light — reflects sunlight. New → crescent → full → crescent → new (29.5 days).

items.label: Mercury
items.detail: Closest to Sun. Smallest. No atmosphere. Extreme temperatures

items.label: Venus
items.detail: Hottest planet. Thick CO₂ atmosphere. Brightest in night sky

items.label: Earth
items.detail: Only planet with life. 71% water. One moon

items.label: Mars
items.detail: Red planet. Two moons. Evidence of ancient water

items.label: Jupiter
items.detail: Largest planet. Great Red Spot storm. 80+ moons

items.label: Saturn
items.detail: Famous rings of ice & rock. Least dense — would float in water

items.label: Uranus
items.detail: Rotates on its side. Pale blue-green colour

items.label: Neptune
items.detail: Farthest planet. Strongest winds in solar system

items.label: Inner (terrestrial)
items.detail: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars — rocky, small, close to Sun

items.label: Outer (gas giants)
items.detail: Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune — gaseous, large, far from Sun

items.label: Asteroid belt
items.detail: Between Mars and Jupiter — thousands of rocky asteroids

right.label: Comets & Meteors
points: Comets: icy, long orbit, glowing tail near Sun
Meteors: space rock burning in atmosphere

flows.title: Why the Moon Changes Shape
flows.description: We see different fractions of the sunlit half as the Moon orbits Earth.

steps.label: New Moon
steps.description: Moon between Earth and Sun — dark side faces us.

steps.label: Waxing crescent
steps.description: Moon moves — small sunlit portion visible on right.

steps.label: First quarter
steps.description: Right half illuminated — 7 days after new moon.

steps.label: Full Moon
steps.description: Earth between Sun and Moon — entire face illuminated.

steps.label: Waning
steps.description: Lit portion shrinks back to new moon — 29.5 day cycle complete.

flows.title: How Planets Orbit the Sun
flows.description: All planets orbit the Sun in the same direction, on nearly flat planes.

steps.label: Sun
steps.description: Sun's gravity holds entire solar system together.

steps.label: Inner planets
steps.description: Mercury to Mars — rocky, close, orbit in months/years.

steps.label: Asteroid belt
steps.description: Between Mars and Jupiter — leftover building blocks.

steps.label: Gas giants
steps.description: Jupiter to Neptune — massive, take decades to orbit.

steps.label: Gravity
steps.description: Sun's gravitational pull balances planets' tendency to fly straight.

flows.title: A Comet's Journey
flows.description: Comets develop tails only when they approach the Sun.

steps.label: Far from Sun
steps.description: Comet in outer solar system — dormant, no tail.

steps.label: Approaches Sun
steps.description: Elliptical orbit brings comet closer to Sun.

steps.label: Sublimation
steps.description: Ices vaporise directly — gas and dust released.

steps.label: Tail forms
steps.description: Two tails: dust (curved) and ion (straight away from Sun).

steps.label: Returns
steps.description: Comet cools again — tail disappears until next orbit.

keyTerms.definition: The Sun and all objects gravitationally bound to it
keyTerms.analogy: The Sun's family of planets and space rocks

keyTerms.definition: Group of stars forming a recognisable pattern
keyTerms.analogy: Dot-to-dot pictures in the night sky — Orion, Ursa Major

keyTerms.definition: Distance light travels in one year (~9.46 trillion km)
keyTerms.analogy: Cosmic measuring tape for distances between stars
A planet's loyal follower in orbit

demos.title: Moon Phases — Sunlit Half Always
demos.description: As the Moon orbits Earth, we see different fractions of its sunlit side.

steps.label: New moon
steps.description: Moon between Earth and Sun — dark side faces us.

steps.label: Waxing phases
steps.description: Moon moves — we see a growing crescent then half-moon.

steps.label: Full moon
steps.description: Earth between Sun and Moon — entire sunlit face visible.

chapterSummary.overview: The night sky holds stars, constellations, planets, moons, and other celestial bodies. This chapter covers the solar system, phases of the moon, eclipses, meteors and comets, artificial satellites, and how ancient and modern astronomers studied the heavens.
keyPoints: Solar system: Sun + 8 planets, dwarf planets, asteroids, comets, meteors.
Planets revolve around the Sun; moons revolve around planets; all held by gravity.
Moon phases caused by how much of the sunlit half we see as it orbits Earth.
Solar eclipse: Moon blocks Sun; Lunar eclipse: Earth blocks sunlight to Moon.
Pole Star indicates north; constellations like Orion and Ursa Major help navigation.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Explain moon phases with diagrams. Differentiate solar vs lunar eclipse. Name planets in order from the Sun.
